betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Arsenic§f

§aA poisonous element that replaces bismuth in circuitry — cost-effective alternative for mass production!§f

§bArsenic§f (As, atomic number 33) is an uncommon material mostly produced in nugget form. Its most common use is making §bArsenic Bronze§f, and it can replace bismuth in the Versatile Integrated Circuit recipe to cut costs.

§6Production§f (§bOre Acidizer§f):
§b• 100 mB§f High-Performance Solvent
§b• 16×§f Oily Scraps
§b→ §eArsenic Nugget§f

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• Arsenic Bronze§f — alloy with copper
§b• Versatile Integrated Circuit§f — replaces bismuth nugget, enabling cheaper circuit mass production

§7[Bismuth Recipe]§f:
Insulator ×3
Printed Silicon Wafer + Bismuth Nugget + Printed Silicon Wafer
Copper/Gold Wire ×3

§7[Arsenic Recipe]§f:
Same pattern but §bArsenic Nugget§f replaces Bismuth Nugget

§eTip§f: Oily Scraps are produced from oil processing. If you have a well-established oil refinery, you can mass-produce arsenic in the Ore Acidizer. Arsenic circuits are slightly less efficient but much cheaper per unit than bismuth circuits!
